zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk於1295年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾時，他的父親Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk52歲，母親Christina Cameron50歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1346年在英國蘇格蘭享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk于1295年出生在英国苏格兰培爾时，他的父亲Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk52岁，母亲Christina Cameron50岁。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1346年在英国苏格兰享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: that
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Hoa, Binh, Hoang, Vinh, Huong, Quang, Dung, Long, Duc.  Southeast Asian Hong, Chi, Ho, Sang, Chu, Lai, On, Kheng, Ku, Kwan, Lim, Lung, Tam, Nam, Tay, Thai, Tham, Manh, Pai, Sieu, Sinh, Tau, Tea, Ti.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 邰, see   Tai   1.
Vietnamese: variant of Sái, see Sai 6.

variants.name: Tai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Kam, Ming, Cheung, Chi Sing, Han, Hong, Hsing, Man, On, Pin, Ping.  Korean Hak, Nam, Woon, Cho, Chong, Chung, Heeyoung, Kok, Min, Pang, Tam, Wook.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 邰: (i) from the placename Tai (邰), the name of a fief (located in Wugong in Shaanxi province) granted to Hou Ji, an official during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ). (ii) complicated form of the Chinese character Tai (台), the name of a place (located in Shandong province) in the state of Lu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (iii) adopted as a surname by the Da Li Ji (大利稽) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Ilk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilczak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Danuta, Stanislawa, Witold, Wojciech.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name   Wilk  .

variants.name: Wilkes
meanings: English (West Midlands): either a variant of Wilk , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, or from the Middle English personal name Wilcus, an uncommon pet form of a personal name in Wil-. The suffix is a double diminutive -k + -us, the latter being used with Old English female names (compare Aldous ). So Wilcus may also be a pet form of an Old English female name such as Wilburg. Alternatively, it may have been used with Norman personal names of either gender, for example as a pet form of William . If Wilcus became an inherited surname it would quickly have become merged with Wilkes. Wilcus may also have been absorbed into Wildgoose .
Dutch and North German: patronymic from the personal name Wilke, a pet form of Will.

variants.name: Wilkosz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Henryk.
Polish: derivative of   Wilk  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Wilczek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Casimir, Ewa, Krzystof, Miroslaw, Zosia.  German Gunther, Natascha.
Polish: from a diminutive of the personal name Wilk , from wilk ‘wolf’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk was born in 1295, in Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk, was 52 and his mother, Christina Cameron, was 50. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1346, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk est né en 1295, à Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Christina Cameron, avait 50 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1346, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk 1295 in Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk 52 und seine Mutter Christina Cameron 50. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1346 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk è nato il nel 1295 nel Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Christina Cameron, aveva 50 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1346, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilkが1295年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアで生まれた時，父Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilkは52歳，母Christina Cameronは50歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1346年にイギリススコットランドで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk(이)가 1295년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina Cameron(은)는 50세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1346년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1346, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk родился в 1295 г. в местности пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Christina Cameron, 50. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1346 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Inchmartin of that Ilk nació en 1295, en Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Alexander Inchmartin of that Ilk, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Christina Cameron, tenía 50 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1346, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años.
